page: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 4096.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per physical core. Local storage consists of a 3800 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and the network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory performance. The server achieved a strong rating in stress-ng multi-core CPU testing and a top-tier PassMark CPU score, driven by strong results in extended instructions and encryption tests. Memory bandwidth is also highly rated, reaching 311.12 GB/sec for large-block read-only operations and 120.27 GB/sec for large-block write operations. However, single-core performance remains average, as shown by the stress-ng single-core score of 1908.7 and Geekbench single-core score of 1670.0.

The hardware configuration is optimized for memory-intensive workloads, showing strong capabilities in database operations and Redis SET operations. While it handles prompt processing for small and medium LLM models with strong throughput, text generation for large 70B models is weak at 2.63 tokens per second due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware. This server is suited for large-scale in-memory databases, memory-intensive enterprise applications, and high-throughput data processing workloads that benefit from massive RAM capacity and high network throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server configured with 128 vCPUs from Intel Xeon 8375C processors and 4096.0 GB of DDR4 memory. It includes a 3800 GB NVMe SSD local instance store and a 100 Gbps baseline network interface. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU and encryption performance, alongside strong memory bandwidth and database operation throughput. However, single-core processing and large-scale LLM text generation are relatively limited, with text generation for a 70B model rated as weak. This server is designed for memory-intensive enterprise applications, large in-memory databases, and high-throughput data processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 128 vCPUs, 4096 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads and databases.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs, 4096 GB RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal, memory-optimized platform featuring four terabytes of RAM and high-speed local NVMe storage for enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz
4096 GB of DDR4 memory providing 32 GB per physical core
3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and encryption capabilities
Strong performance for in-memory databases and Redis operations
Weak text generation speeds for large-scale language models due to lack of GPU acceleration
